perturbation.original_text: (f) “Personal Data” has the same meaning assigned to this phrase as in Section 2(1) of the Personal Data Protection Act 2012 (No. 26 of 2012) of the Statutes of the Republic of Singapore .
perturbation.changed_text: (f) “Personal Data” means any information relating to an individual.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Personal Data' explicitly references the Personal Data Protection Act 2012 of Singapore. Changing this to a generic definition creates a contradiction with Clause 4.1(a), which requires full compliance with the Act. Now the contract vaguely defines the term when needing to comply with specific regulations, because the changed definition doesn't enforce this. This creates an in-text contradiction as 4.1(a) cannot be enforced due to the changed in location 1.1(f)
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 The Company shall, in relation to Personal Data: - (a) ensure that it has, in relation to all Personal Data obtained and/or collected by it , fully complied with all req uirements of the Personal Data Protection Act (No. 26 of 2012); (b) process Personal Data only in accordance with the written instructions given by IHiS and to such extent necessary and appropriate for the completion of the Purpose ; (c) promptly deal with any enqu iry from IHiS relating to the Company’s processing of Personal Data; (d) not transfer or allow the Personal Data to be transferred, outside of Singapore, unless expressly instructed or authorised by IHiS; and (e) provide all necessary co -operation and assistance ( whether to IHiS or otherwise) to allow access and/or correction of Personal Data in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act 2012 .
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly defined 'Personal Data' with reference to Singapore's Personal Data Protection Act 2012. The changed text replaces this specific definition with a generic one ('any information relating to an individual'). This creates a direct contradiction with clause 4.1(a), which mandates full compliance with the Personal Data Protection Act (No. 26 of 2012). Because the changed text no longer enforces compliance with the specific act mentioned in the original text, clause 4.1(a) is now unenforceable, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction, according to the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 The Company shall, in relation to Personal Data: - (a) ensure that it has, in relation to all Personal Data obtained and/or collected by it , fully complied with all req uirements of the Personal Data Protection Act (No. 26 of 2012);
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 The Company shall, in relation to Personal Data: - (a) ensure that it has, in relation to all Personal Data obtained and/or collected by it , complied with internal company policies;
perturbation.explanation: Changing the requirement to comply with the Personal Data Protection Act to complying with 'internal company policies' directly contradicts the original intention of protecting personal data under a legal framework. This creates uncertainty because section 4.2(d) refers to compliance with PDPA Documentation which refers to Personal Data but the definition from 4.1(a) its being reffered to interal policies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Without prejudice to Clause 4.1 above, the Company shall take all reasonable measures to ensure: (a) that any Personal Data belonging to IHiS or its Affiliates which is held by the Company is protected against loss, unauthorised access, use, modification, disclosure or other misuse, and that only authorised personnel have access to that Personal Data; (b) that, to the extent t hat the Personal Data is no longer required by the Company for legal or business purposes, that Personal Data is destroyed or re -delivered to IHiS in accordance with Clause 5 below; (c) that IHiS is immediately alerted in writing (with full particulars) of any unauthorised access, disclosure or other breach of this Clause 4 and the Company undertakes, as soon as reasonably practicable, all steps to prevent further unauthorised access, disc losure or other breach of this c lause (including providing IHiS with such reports or information concerning such steps as and when requested by IHiS); and (d) it keeps itself appraised of any and all notices and circulars which IHiS may from time to time notify to the Company, including without limitation any policies, guidelines, circulars or notices relating to personal data (“PDPA Documentation”), and to perform its duties or discharge its liabilities in connection with the Purpose in a manner which is consistent with the PDPA Documentation, and will not cause IHiS to be in breac h of the same. For the purposes of t his clause , the Company hereby expressly acknowledges and agrees that it has read the PDPA Documentation and is aware of and will compensate IHiS for any and all potential loss and damage caused to IHiS and/or its Affili ates arising from or in connection with any breach of thi s clause.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates compliance with the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA), a legal framework. The changed text replaces this with compliance with 'internal company policies.' This directly contradicts clause 4.2(d), which refers to 'PDPA Documentation' and requires the company to act consistently with it. By changing the foundation from legal compliance to internal policies, the company may no longer be compliant with the PDPA, thus contradicting the requirement in 4.2(d) to adhere to 'PDPA Documentation' and avoid causing IHiS to breach the same.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 The Company shall within seven days of : (f) completion of the Purpose ; or (g) receipt of a written request from IHiS; or (h) expiry or termination of the Agreement, return to IHiS all documents and materials (and all copies thereof) containing the IHiS’ Confidential Information or destroy the same, and certify in writing to IHiS that it has complied with the requirements of this sub -clause . Notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose or return of the documents and materials as aforesaid, the Company shall continue to be bound by the undertakings set out in Clause s 3 and 4 above .
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 The Company shall within seven days of : (f) completion of the Purpose ; or (g) receipt of a written request from IHiS; or (h) expiry or termination of the Agreement, return to IHiS all documents and materials (and all copies thereof) containing the IHiS’ Confidential Information or destroy the same, and certify in writing to IHiS that it has complied with the requirements of this sub -clause .
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose or return of the documents and materials as aforesaid, the Company shall continue to be bound by the undertakings set out in Clause s 3 and 4 above', the contract now implies that the confidentiality obligations cease upon completion of the Purpose or return of the documents. This contradicts the fundamental nature of a non-disclosure agreement, where confidentiality is expected to survive the agreement's term. The intext contradiction that arises because without it clause 3 and 4 obligations would cease to apply
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 In consideration of the foregoing and the exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information by IHiS , the Company undertakes in relation to IHiS’ Confidential Informatio n: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose; (b) not to make any commercial use thereof; (c) not to use the same for the ben efit of itself or of any third party other than pursuant to a further agreement with IHiS; (d) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any information, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including with out limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one of the exceptions in Clause 1.1(c) ; (e) not to copy, reproduce , reverse engineer or reduce to writing any part thereof except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose and that any copies, reproductions or reductions to writing so made shall be the property of IHiS; (f) not to disclose the same whethe r to its employees or to third parties except in confidence to such of its Representatives who have been informed of the confi dential nature thereof and who need to know the same for the Purpose and that: (i) such Representatives are obliged by their contracts of employment or service not to disclose the same or to use the same otherwise than for the Purpose ; and (ii) the Company shall enforce such obligations at its expense and at the request of IHiS in so far as breach thereof relates to IHiS' Confidential Information; (g) to be responsible for the performance of sub -clauses (a) to (f) above on the part
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that confidentiality obligations in Clauses 3 and 4 continue even after the completion of the Purpose or return of documents. The changed text omits this 'Notwithstanding' clause, implying that these confidentiality obligations cease upon completion of the Purpose or return of documents. This directly contradicts Clause 3.1, which outlines the Company's ongoing confidentiality undertakings, creating a strong in-text contradiction because now clause 3 and 4 obligations would cease to apply when they are in fact expected to persist.

perturbation.original_text: 7.1 The Company acknowledge s that the Confidential Information has been developed or obtained by IHiS through the investment of significant time, effort and expense, and that such Confidential Information provides IHiS with a significant competitive advantage over its competitors. The Company understand s and agree s that any breach of this Agreement , as wel l as any unauthorised processing, collection, access, use or disclosure of Personal Data by the Company, will result in immediate and irreparable harm to IHiS and its Affiliates and that monetary damages may not be an adequate remedy in the event of such a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. Accordingly, the Company agree s that IHiS shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance, in the event of a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement in addition to all other remedies available to IHiS seeking remedy at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.1 The Company acknowledge s that the Confidential Information has been developed or obtained by IHiS through significant investment. The Company understand s and agree s that any breach of this Agreement will result in harm to IHiS and its Affiliates and that monetary damages may not be an adequate remedy in the event of such a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. Accordingly, the Company agree s that IHiS shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance, in the event of a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement in addition to all other remedies available to IHiS seeking remedy at law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrases 'effort and expense, and that such Confidential Information provides IHiS with a significant competitive advantage over its competitors' and 'as well as any unauthorized processing, collection, access, use or disclosure of Personal Data by the Company', the altered section weakens the justification for equitable relief. This now contradicts and undermines the claim that harm to IHiS is 'immediate and irreparable', creating an in-text contradiction with the subsequent claim that monetary damages might be inadequate. The omitted text weakens the equitable relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 The Company acknowledge s that the Confidential Information has been developed or obtained by IHiS through significant investment. The Company understand s and agree s that any breach of this Agreement will result in harm to IHiS and its Affiliates and that monetary damages may not be an adequate remedy in the event of such a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. Accordingly, the Company agree s that IHiS shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance, in the event of a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement in addition to all other remedies available to IHiS seeking remedy at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase 'effort and expense, and that such Confidential Information provides IHiS with a significant competitive advantage over its competitors' from Section 7.1. It also removes 'as well as any unauthorized processing, collection, access, use or disclosure of Personal Data by the Company'. According to the perturbation explanation, this weakens the justification for equitable relief, contradicting the claim that harm to IHiS is 'immediate and irreparable'. Since both the changed text and contradicted text are the same, the removal of these phrases creates a contradiction in terms of the equitable relief that IHiS is entitled to, since the change reduces the magnitude of harm IHiS would suffer.
